summary: Add the same validIdentifierRE.MatchString(field) guard ListField uses, so ResolveByName cannot fmt.Sprintf an unvalidated field name into the json_extract SQL path.
reason: Greptile P1 (security) on PR #533. ListField and ResolveByName were intended to mirror each other (the in-file comment at store.go:1966 states this), but the generator emitted the validIdentifierRE guard only on ListField. Today's callers all pass literal identifiers like "name"/"email", so blast radius is bounded — but the asymmetry is the real smell and the guard belongs there as defense in depth.
validated_outcome: ResolveByName now silently skips any matchFields value that does not look like a SQL identifier; legitimate callers (passing "name"/"key"/"email") behave identically.
